forecast-period.text: A high pressure system over the Tasman Sea is responsible for a ridge hovering over the north of the state. A cold front will slide over the south of SA overnight, while the ridge persists into Thursday. Northerly winds will freshen ahead of a second cold front expected overnight Friday or Saturday morning.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Tuesday for Gulf St Vincent
forecast-period.text: Northerly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots in the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres south of Port Vincent to Brighton.
West to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Partly cloudy. 70% chance of showers.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Wednesday for Gulf St Vincent
forecast-period.text: North to northwesterly 15 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots south of Port Vincent to Brighton early in the morning. Winds tending west to northwesterly 15 to 20 knots in the early morning then becoming variable about 10 knots in the morning.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning, then decreasing below 1 metre around midday.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
Northeasterly about 10 knots increasing to 10 to 15 knots in the evening.
Below 1 metre.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre.
Becoming cloudy.
Northeasterly 10 to 15 knots turning northerly 15 to 25 knots during the morning.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1 to 2 metres during the morning.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Partly cloudy. 70% chance of showers.
